name: Producer Bank Of Texas
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Central and Southeast Texas with business and personal banking services, rooted in Powell State Bank's 1910 heritage.
description_long: Producer Bank of Texas was formed through the acquisition of Powell State Bank in 2024, combining over a century of community banking history with experienced native Texan bankers. The bank positions itself as a local alternative to larger institutions, emphasizing fast decision-making and personalized service for business owners and families in Central and Southeast Texas. The company offers comprehensive banking services including business banking with working capital solutions, personal banking for individuals and families, mobile banking technology, bill pay services, and fraud prevention resources. Producer Bank distinguishes itself through its focus on local market knowledge, independent decision-making authority, entrepreneurial spirit, and commitment to supporting creators, developers, builders, and producers in the Texas economy. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing standard deposit protection. While the company emphasizes being a "local bank with resources of a big bank," the website provides limited specific details about product offerings, interest rates, fee structures, or geographic service area beyond Central and Southeast Texas. As a community bank focused on business lending and local relationships, it may not be ideal for customers seeking a wide range of specialized financial products or national branch networks.
services: Business banking with working capital and complicated deal financing
Mobile banking app with digital account management
Checking and savings accounts (referenced on homepage)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Local decision-making with fast approval timelines for business lending
Deep community roots dating back to Powell State Bank's founding in 1910
Mobile banking app available for remote account access and management
Dedicated business banking division understanding independent business owner needs
Bill pay services integrated into online banking platform
Fraud prevention education and internal account protection procedures
cons: Limited geographic service area (Central and Southeast Texas only, not national)
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, fees, or product details
No transparent pricing or rate comparison information available online
Minimal detail on personal banking products, loan types, or credit offerings
Small community bank with potentially fewer branches and ATMs than larger competitors
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs in Central and Southeast Texas seeking personalized lending
Families and individuals in Texas wanting community bank service with local decision-makers
Business owners with complex financing needs preferring relationship-based banking
diagnosis: Producer Bank of Texas is best suited for business owners and families in Central/Southeast Texas seeking personalized, fast local lending decisions from a community-focused FDIC-insured bank. The primary caveat is the limited geographic footprint and lack of transparent product details and pricing on their website, requiring direct contact for specific rates and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
